_meta.note: Curated, non-sectarian short adhkar suitable for a ~20s break. Arabic is fully vocalized and treated as immutable. REQUIRES scholarly/editorial sign-off before public release (see docs/specs and the asset provenance process).
_meta.transliterationScheme: Light ALA-LC-style, no special characters, approachable for repetition.

dhikr.virtue: Light on the tongue, heavy on the scale, beloved to the Most Merciful.
It fills the scale of good deeds.

dhikr.english: There is no deity worthy of worship except Allah.

dhikr.virtue: Among the most beloved words to Allah.

dhikr.english: Glory be to Allah and praise be to Him.
dhikr.virtue: Whoever says it 100 times a day, his sins are wiped away though they be like the foam of the sea.

dhikr.arabic: سُبْحَانَ اللّٰهِ وَبِحَمْدِهِ، سُبْحَانَ اللّٰهِ الْعَظِيمِ
dhikr.english: Glory and praise be to Allah; glory be to Allah the Magnificent.
dhikr.virtue: Two phrases light on the tongue, heavy on the scale, beloved to the Most Merciful.

dhikr.english: I seek the forgiveness of Allah.
dhikr.virtue: The Prophet ﷺ sought forgiveness more than seventy times a day.

dhikr.arabic: لَا حَوْلَ وَلَا قُوَّةَ إِلَّا بِاللّٰهِ
dhikr.transliteration: La hawla wa la quwwata illa billah
dhikr.english: There is no might nor power except with Allah.
dhikr.virtue: A treasure from the treasures of Paradise.

dhikr.arabic: اللّٰهُمَّ صَلِّ وَسَلِّمْ عَلَىٰ نَبِيِّنَا مُحَمَّدٍ
dhikr.transliteration: Allahumma salli wa sallim ‘ala nabiyyina Muhammad
dhikr.english: O Allah, send blessings and peace upon our Prophet Muhammad.
dhikr.reference: Sahih Muslim 408 (on the virtue of salawat)
dhikr.virtue: Whoever sends blessings upon the Prophet ﷺ once, Allah sends ten upon him.
